2021-02-01.title: Thailand Targets Pro-Democracy Protesters in Sweeping Legal Dragnet
2021-02-01.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of people marched in student-led demonstrations last year. Now some face decades behind bars.

2021-02-01.title: Myanmar’s Protests, Explained
2021-02-01.abstract: Weeks of demonstrations have roiled the nation since a Feb. 1 coup brought back full military rule following years of quasi-democracy. Here is what we know.

2021-02-01.title: One Case, Total Lockdown: Australia’s Lessons for a Pandemic World
2021-02-01.abstract: The country’s short, sharp responses have repeatedly subdued the virus and allowed a return to near normalcy. Now its model is being applied to Perth, its fourth-largest city.

2021-02-01.title: The Chinese police arrest more than 80 people suspected of manufacturing fake vaccines.
2021-02-01.abstract: The police in China have broken up a counterfeiting ring that they say manufactured and sold more than 3,000 fake coronavirus vaccines across the country, Xinhua, China’s state-run news agency, reported on Monday.

2021-02-01.title: The ‘Dr. Fauci’ of the Texas border is counting the dead.
2021-02-01.abstract: During January, the pandemic’s deadliest month, Laredo, Texas, held the bleak distinction of having one of the most severe outbreaks of any city in the United States. The death toll in the overwhelmingly Latino city of 277,000 now stands at more than 630 — including at least 126 in January alone.

2021-02-02.title: A study shows the variant spreading rapidly in Britain might make vaccines less effective.
2021-02-02.abstract: The highly infectious variant has developed a worrisome mutation, new research has found.

2021-02-02.title: Moderna wants to increase the number of vaccine doses in each vial.
2021-02-02.abstract: U.S. regulators could decide within a few weeks whether to allow Moderna, the developer of one of the two federally authorized Covid-19 vaccines, to increase the number of doses in its vials — which could accelerate the nation’s vaccination rate.

2021-02-02.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in February
2021-02-02.abstract: Our streaming picks for February, including ‘Parks and Recreation,’ ‘News of the World’ and ‘Bliss.’

2021-02-02.title: First Came the Lockdown. Then Came the Wildfire.
2021-02-02.abstract: Residents on the outskirts of Perth in Western Australia fled their homes in the middle of the night, just days after being told to stay in because of the coronavirus.

2021-02-02.title: A Chinese Dissident Tried to Fly to His Sick Wife in the U.S. Then He Vanished.
2021-02-02.abstract: Friends and family have lost contact with Yang Maodong, who was stopped at a Shanghai airport from joining a flight to San Francisco.

2021-02-03.title: U.N. Delays Salvage of Yemen Oil Tanker Amid Fears of Major Spill
2021-02-03.abstract: The United Nations had planned to start a maritime salvage operation to prevent leaks from a dilapidated tanker holding four times the oil that spilled in the Exxon Valdez disaster. But Houthi insurgents have failed to provide a written security guarantee, the U.N. said.

2021-02-03.title: Hunting Rats, Defending Democracy: Hong Kong’s Local Leaders Under Fire
2021-02-03.abstract: District councilors typically tended to mundane matters like pest control and new bus stops. Now, they’re the last line of defense in keeping the city’s pro-democracy opposition alive.

2021-02-03.title: Nearly half of U.S. states allow teachers to get shots.
2021-02-03.abstract: Almost half of U.S. states have begun allowing teachers to be vaccinated as officials decide which groups should be given priority for early protection against the coronavirus, a New York Times survey shows. By this week, 24 states and Washington, D.C., were providing shots to teachers of kindergarten through high school students.

2021-02-03.title: Rebuild or Leave ‘Paradise’: Climate Change Dilemma Facing a Nicaraguan Coastal Town
2021-02-03.abstract: Two major November hurricanes slammed into the same part of the Nicaraguan coast, laying waste to the Miskito village of Haulover. Faced with a future of intensifying storms, the residents must now consider whether to abandon their way of life by the ocean and move inland.

2021-02-03.title: After Hurricane’s Devastation, a Dilemma in Nicaragua: Rebuild or Relocate?
2021-02-03.abstract: A wedge of ocean the width of a football field now cuts through the middle of their town, leaving villagers with an anguishing question.

2021-02-04.title: Inside the Shadowy Militias Luring Unsuspecting Afghans to Fight, or Die
2021-02-04.abstract: Afghanistan’s poor are being deceived into defending outposts from the Taliban, sometimes under the guise of construction work — a scheme partially bankrolled by the government.

2021-02-04.title: Do viewers want TV that reflects a shared pandemic reality, or that distracts from it?
2021-02-04.abstract: Last June, when the “Grey’s Anatomy” writers’ room reconvened, virtually, after a longer than usual hiatus, Krista Vernoff, the longtime showrunner, asked whether or not the coming season should incorporate the coronavirus pandemic.

2021-02-04.title: Former Uganda Militia Leader Is Convicted of War Crimes
2021-02-04.abstract: Dominic Ongwen was abducted by the Lord’s Resistance Army militia as a child, and later rose to be one of its commanders. He was once a victim of some of the same crimes he was accused of later in life.

2021-02-04.title: Iranian Diplomat Is Convicted in Plot to Bomb Opposition Rally in France
2021-02-04.abstract: A court in Belgium sentenced Assadollah Assadi, an envoy based in Vienna, to 20 years in prison for his role in a thwarted attack on a group that seeks to overthrow the Iranian leadership.

2021-02-04.title: U.K. Regulator Revokes License for China-Backed Broadcaster
2021-02-04.abstract: The regulator, Ofcom, said it would no longer allow China Global Television Network to operate in Britain, in part because of its affiliation with the Chinese Communist Party.

2021-02-05.title: Its Borders Shut, New Zealand Prods Local Tourists to ‘Do Something New’
2021-02-05.abstract: A viral ad campaign urges New Zealanders to find new ways to look at their own backyard — and to stop posting hot-tub vacation photos.

2021-02-05.title: A ‘Masculinity Crisis’? China Says the Boys Are Not All Right
2021-02-05.abstract: The Education Ministry plans to beef up gym classes after a top official said female teachers and pop culture had made boys “weak, inferior and timid.”

2021-02-05.title: T Magazine Australia Launching in March
2021-02-05.abstract: Australians will get a new luxury magazine from The New York Times in March.

2021-02-05.title: Power, Patriotism and 1.4 Billion People: How China Beat the Virus and Roared Back
2021-02-05.abstract: The Chinese Communist Party reached deep into private business and the broader population to drive a recovery, an authoritarian approach that has emboldened its top leader, Xi Jinping.

2021-02-05.title: Pacific Islands’ Most Important Megaphone Falls Into Discord
2021-02-05.abstract: The future of an 18-nation group is in doubt after Palau abandoned it over a leadership dispute.

2021-02-06.title: I.C.C. Rules It Has Jurisdiction to Examine Possible Israel War Crimes
2021-02-06.abstract: The International Criminal Court on Friday said it has the authority to investigate alleged crimes in the occupied territories, in a ruling condemned by Israel and praised by Palestinians.

2021-02-06.title: Public Buildings Set Ablaze in Chile After Police Shoot Street Juggler
2021-02-06.abstract: Protesters were outraged by the killing, which was recorded on video. Ten public offices burned to the ground, leaving a city of almost 34,000 practically without public services.

2021-02-06.title: In Afghanistan, a Booming Kidney Trade Preys on the Poor
2021-02-06.abstract: Widespread poverty and an ambitious private hospital are helping to fuel an illegal market — a portal to new misery for the country’s most vulnerable.

2021-02-06.title: To Fight or Hide: Fear Grips Myanmar With Military Back in Charge
2021-02-06.abstract: Dozens of arrests, beatings by mysterious thugs and telecommunication cutoffs are the new reality across the country. But civil disobedience defiantly persists.

2021-02-06.title: Nominated for a Nobel Peace Prize? Wait Until You Receive It to Brag
2021-02-06.abstract: Thousands of people, including university professors, can submit nominations. Hundreds are submitted per year.

2021-02-07.title: Haiti Braces for Unrest as a Defiant President Refuses to Step Down
2021-02-07.abstract: Haiti is “on the verge of explosion,” with many accusing the government of supporting gangs to stay in power as a constitutional crisis looms.

2021-02-07.title: Glacier Bursts in India, Leaving More Than 100 Missing in Floods
2021-02-07.abstract: Rescue efforts were underway in the northern state of Uttarakhand, where officials said a hydroelectric dam project had been largely swept away.

2021-02-07.title: Ecuador’s Presidential Vote Heads to a Runoff
2021-02-07.abstract: A leftist economist, the protégé of a populist past president, Rafael Correa, was the top vote-getter. Which opponent he will face in April appeared uncertain.

2021-02-07.title: 60 Black health experts urge Black Americans to get vaccinated.
2021-02-07.abstract: Black Americans are being killed by Covid-19 at 1.5 times the rate of white Americans, yet many Black people are hesitant to line up for a vaccine.

2021-02-07.title: How to handle pandemic fear and stress? For many, the answer is getting married.
2021-02-07.abstract: If the world is ending, who do you want by your side?

2021-02-08.title: China Arrests Australian Journalist on Spying Charge
2021-02-08.abstract: The case of Cheng Lei, a CGTN host who was detained in August, has added to tensions between China and Australia.

2021-02-08.title: This Chinese Businesswoman Was a Model of Success. Then She Angered the Party.
2021-02-08.abstract: The authorities have charged Geng Xiaonan with illegal business activities, but her true offense may have been sympathizing with critics of the Communist Party.

2021-02-08.title: Virus Hastens Exit from Israel’s Ultra-Orthodox Community
2021-02-08.abstract: Nationwide lockdowns and school closings have broken routines and left ultra-Orthodox Jews time for questioning and self-discovery. Some found the examined life worth leaving.

2021-02-08.title: Maskless fans celebrate the Super Bowl in Tampa’s streets.
2021-02-08.abstract: Shortly after the Tampa Bay Buccaneers crushed the Kansas City Chiefs in Sunday’s Super Bowl, fans took to the streets of Tampa to celebrate, and few, it appeared from videos of the crowds, were wearing masks, cementing concerns that the event would help spread the coronavirus.

2021-02-08.title: Democrats plan a child tax credit of up to $3,600 as part of their stimulus bill.
2021-02-08.abstract: Top House Democrats are preparing to introduce legislation that would send up to $3,600 per child to millions of Americans, as lawmakers aim to change the tax code to target child poverty rates as part of President Biden’s sweeping $1.9 trillion stimulus package.

2021-02-09.title: Colombia Makes ‘Historic’ Decision to Grant Legal Status to 1.7 Million Venezuelan Migrants
2021-02-09.abstract: President Iván Duque announced Monday that the Venezuelan migrants who fled an economic collapse at home can legally stay in Colombia for 10 years if they register with the authorities.

2021-02-09.title: A Glimpse of a Bygone Life on Scottish Islands, Plucked From the Trash
2021-02-09.abstract: Hundreds of pictures taken decades ago in the Shetland Islands, off northern Scotland, were saved from being thrown away. Now, they are finding new life online.

2021-02-09.title: Solidarity isn’t an easy sell in Europe, as the E.U. lags in the vaccine race.
2021-02-09.abstract: As the European Union began its campaign to line up vaccines, it was slower off the mark, focused on prices while the United States and Britain made money no object, and succumbed to an abundance of regulatory caution. All of those things have left the bloc flat-footed as drugmakers fall behind on their promised orders.

2021-02-09.title: At the Australian Open, the sports world flirts with normality.
Bodies again pile up in Bolivia as Latin America endures a long, deadly coronavirus wave.
2021-02-09.abstract: “When 10 or 20 patients die, their beds are full again in a few hours,” one specialist said.

2021-02-10.title: Another antibody therapy is authorized for high-risk Covid-19 patients.
2021-02-10.abstract: Researchers hope Eli Lilly’s new combination therapy will be better able to fight virus variants than a similar treatment already in use.

2021-02-10.title: He Calls the Tie a ‘Colonial Noose.’ Now Parliament Says It’s No Longer Mandatory.
2021-02-10.abstract: Rawiri Waititi, a Maori politician in New Zealand, was kicked out of Parliament for refusing to wear a tie as a marker of Indigenous resistance.

2021-02-10.title: France Stalls Between Stubbornly High Infection Rates and Slow Vaccine Rollout
2021-02-10.abstract: Coronavirus numbers have plateaued despite months of economically damaging restrictions, and vaccinations still lag behind other European countries, leaving many French with an uneasy feeling of being stuck.

2021-02-10.title: U.K. residents may not be allowed to travel abroad until all adults are vaccinated.
2021-02-10.abstract: Britain’s transportation secretary said it was too early to tell whether people would be allowed to take vacations this year, either at home or abroad.

2021-02-10.title: Childhood colds don’t prevent coronavirus infection, a study finds.
2021-02-10.abstract: The theory was compelling: Could children be less vulnerable to the new coronavirus because they carry antibodies to other coronaviruses that cause the common cold? Might that also help explain why some people infected with the new virus have mild symptoms while others are more severely affected?

2021-02-11.title: The Newest Diplomatic Currency: Covid-19 Vaccines
2021-02-11.abstract: India, China, the U.A.E. and others dole out donations in countries where they seek sway. In some cases, they are sending doses despite pressing needs at home.

2021-02-11.title: These fake masks looked real, and sold by the millions.
Massachusetts will vaccinate companions who accompany older people to appointments.
2021-02-11.abstract: In a bid to get more residents age 75 and older vaccinated, Massachusetts officials say they will also inoculate the people accompanying them, regardless of age, to mass vaccination sites, which can be confusing to navigate.

2021-02-11.title: Vaccines are the new diplomatic currency, and other news from around the world.
2021-02-11.abstract: It’s one of the world’s most in-demand commodities and has become a new currency for international diplomacy: Countries with the means or the know-how are using coronavirus vaccines to curry favor or thaw frosty relations.

2021-02-12.title: A U.S. Sportswriter’s ‘Glimpse of the Future’ in Near-Normal Australia
2021-02-12.abstract: He was curious to know how it felt to cover a global event that is giving us a glimpse into a post-pandemic world.

2021-02-12.title: As the Australian Open plays on, Victoria officials order a ‘circuit breaker’ Covid lockdown.
2021-02-12.abstract: The tournament will continue, though without spectators, as professional athletes are deemed essential workers under new restrictions in Victoria state.

2021-02-12.title: Donald Trump’s bout with Covid-19 was more serious than acknowledged at the time.
2021-02-12.abstract: President Donald J. Trump was sicker with Covid-19 in October than publicly acknowledged at the time, with extremely depressed blood oxygen levels at one point and a lung problem associated with pneumonia caused by the coronavirus, according to four people familiar with his condition.

2021-02-12.title: Biden administration steps in to help with the supply chain for N95 masks.
2021-02-12.abstract: The White House on Thursday stepped into the fragmented market for disposable virus-filtering N95 masks, moving to connect medical supply companies with mask manufacturers who say they are loaded with inventory but can’t find buyers — even in the midst of a pandemic in which doctors and nurses are desperate for the protective gear.

2021-02-13.title: International Court, Battered by Critics, Elects Briton as New Prosecutor
2021-02-13.abstract: Karim Khan, a veteran of the international legal scene who has worked on both the defense and prosecution sides, will shape the court’s image and effectiveness for years to come.

2021-02-13.title: California extends vaccine eligibility to people with disabilities after backlash.
2021-02-13.abstract: The state will extend Covid-19 vaccinations to people over 16 who are debilitated or immunocompromised by cancer and other diseases and chronic conditions.

2021-02-13.title: With Pig Parades, Once-Feared Opposition Party in Taiwan Tries a Revamp
2021-02-13.abstract: The Beijing-friendly party, which long held an iron grip on the island, is struggling to stay relevant at a time when many residents are wary of China’s ambitions.

2021-02-13.title: Australia’s ‘Rebel Reverend’ Goes Viral With Barbed Liberal Messages
2021-02-13.abstract: The Rev. Rod Bower became a national sensation with pugnacious political signs shared by millions over social media. Now he’s questioning the internet dynamics that made him famous.

2021-02-13.title: In Sudan Border Town, Desperate Ethiopians Find ‘Second Mother Country’
2021-02-13.abstract: Tens of thousands of Christian refugees, fleeing the violence in the Tigray region of Ethiopia, have been given a warm welcome by the residents of a sleepy Sudanese town: “We are brothers.”

2021-02-14.title: Duterte’s Forces Have a New Target: University Students
2021-02-14.abstract: The government in the Philippines has announced a decision to end a 32-year agreement barring security forces from a prestigious campus. Students say they won’t be intimidated.

2021-02-14.title: China’s Crackdown on Muslims Extends to a Resort Island
2021-02-14.abstract: The Utsuls of Hainan island were once celebrated by the government for their links to the larger Muslim world. Not anymore.

2021-02-14.title: A Decade After the Arab Spring, Autocrats Still Rule the Mideast
2021-02-14.abstract: The popular uprisings of 2011 mostly failed, but they gave the region a taste for democracy that continues to whet an appetite for change.

2021-02-14.title: Japan approves its first Covid vaccine, opting for Pfizer-BioNTech’s.
2021-02-14.abstract: Though infection rates are relatively low in the country, Japan is unlikely to have its full population vaccinated before the delayed 2020 Olympic Games open there this summer.

2021-02-14.title: ‘What’s the Point?’ Young People’s Despair Deepens as Covid-19 Crisis Drags On
2021-02-14.abstract: Experts paint a grim picture of the struggle with lockdown isolation — a “mental health pandemic” that should be treated as seriously as containing the coronavirus.

2021-02-15.title: In a Dangerous Game of Cat and Mouse, Iran Eyes New Targets in Africa
2021-02-15.abstract: Fifteen people arrested in Ethiopia were part of what American and Israeli officials said was a foiled Iranian plot against diplomats from the United Arab Emirates.

2021-02-15.title: The Taliban Close In on Afghan Cities, Pushing the Country to the Brink
2021-02-15.abstract: The Taliban have positioned themselves around several major population centers, including the capital of Kandahar Province, as the Biden administration weighs whether to withdraw or to stay.

2021-02-15.title: States in the U.S. are expanding access to vaccines. The supply isn’t keeping up.
2021-02-15.abstract: Experts say expanding eligibility requires a delicate balance of prioritizing those most at risk and ensuring no dose goes to waste.

2021-02-15.title: Parliament Rape Claim Roils Australian Government
2021-02-15.abstract: A former government staff member said she had been made to choose between going to the police and keeping her job. Prime Minister Scott Morrison apologized.

2021-02-15.title: ‘We Can Bring Down the Regime’: Myanmar’s Protesting Workers Are Unbowed
2021-02-15.abstract: Two weeks after the military took power in a coup, growing work stoppages are undermining the ruling generals’ attempt to assert authority over an angry population.

2021-02-16.title: Rocket Attack in Iraq Kills a U.S. Military Contractor
2021-02-16.abstract: Some saw the barrage of rockets aimed at an airport in the northern city of Erbil as a test of the new administration in Washington.

2021-02-16.title: The Haunted House of Soviets Gets a New Life
2021-02-16.abstract: Residents of Kaliningrad, Russia’s westernmost region, search for meaning in a building many see as an egregious architectural mistake. “It’s ugly, but it’s ours.”

2021-02-16.title: European Court Backs Germany in Case Over 2009 Killings of Afghan Civilians
2021-02-16.abstract: The European Court of Human Rights ruled Germany sufficiently investigated a bombing in Afghanistan that killed as many as 90 civilians.

2021-02-16.title: Bangladesh Court Sentences Five to Death for Killing American Blogger
2021-02-16.abstract: Avijit Roy, a critic of religious extremism, was hacked to death by machete-wielding assailants in February 2015.

2021-02-16.title: In Turkey’s Safe Zone in Syria, Security and Misery Go Hand in Hand
2021-02-16.abstract: Turkey was widely criticized when it sent forces into Syria, but today its soldiers stand between millions of Syrians and potential slaughter at the hands of President Bashar al-Assad’s forces.

2021-02-17.title: Your Wednesday Briefing
2021-02-17.abstract: Sprawling winter storms across the U.S.

2021-02-17.title: North Korean in Diving Gear Crosses Border Into South Korea Undetected
2021-02-17.abstract: The South Korean military admitted to a lapse in its border security. The crossing marked the second embarrassing breach for the country in recent months.

2021-02-17.title: ‘How Many Funerals Will Come Out of This One?’
2021-02-17.abstract: Suspicious of secular authority, Israel’s ultra-Orthodox Jews have often flouted antivirus protocols. Wary of hospitals, many prefer to stay home when they do get sick. A look inside an insular culture that has been both a vector for the virus and its victim.

2021-02-17.title: Indian Court Clears Journalist of Defamation Claim in #MeToo Case
2021-02-17.abstract: Priya Ramani had faced up to two years in prison after making sexual harassment accusations against M.J. Akbar, once a prominent member of the Modi government.

2021-02-17.title: Europe’s next pandemic question: How to pay for it all.
2021-02-17.abstract: Billions of euros are being deployed to nationalize payrolls, suppress bankruptcies and avoid mass unemployment as Europe battles the pandemic. Trillions more are being earmarked for stimulus to stoke a desperately needed recovery.

2021-02-18.title: ‘You Are Not Alone’: Spanish Rapper’s Arrest Sparks Free Speech Protests
2021-02-18.abstract: Pablo Hasél had been sentenced to prison after a conviction on charges he voiced support for insurgents and insulted an ex-king. His case set off a national debate over free speech.

2021-02-18.title: New Zealand to Roll Out Free Period Products to All Students
2021-02-18.abstract: The program, designed to reduce “period poverty,” will begin in June.

2021-02-18.title: After Leader’s Sexist Remark, Tokyo Olympics Makes Symbolic Shift
2021-02-18.abstract: The selection of Seiko Hashimoto as president represented an abrupt turn after the Olympic committee had apparently intended to name another man in his 80s.

2021-02-18.title: ‘Special and Beautiful’ Whistled Language Echoes Around This Island
2021-02-18.abstract: On a jagged island in the Canary archipelago, a whistling language known as “Silbo Gomero” is still in use thanks to mandatory classes for schoolchildren.

2021-02-19.title: Israel and Syria Complete a Prisoner Swap, Mediated by Russia
2021-02-19.abstract: An Israeli woman in her 20s was allowed to leave Syria for Russia, after Israel returned two captured shepherds to Syria.

2021-02-19.title: Feathers and Female Power: The Costumes of Carnival
2021-02-19.abstract: A vibrant, skimpy ensemble worn at a Caribbean Carnival is so much more than a visual delight: “It’s a celebration of women’s glory in themselves.”

2021-02-19.title: What Is Missed When Carnival Is Canceled: Sun, Joy, Freedom
2021-02-19.abstract: Loyal enthusiasts on what they missed in a year they needed Carnival the most.

2021-02-19.title: The Fun Police: Law Enforcement Comes to Carnival
2021-02-19.abstract: Events like J’Ouvert in New York and Carnival in London have seen violent incidents. But the way they are policed says a lot about what happens when Black people gather.

2021-02-19.title: How a Trinidadian Communist Invented London’s Biggest Party
2021-02-19.abstract: The Notting Hill Carnival was canceled last year. But it likely wouldn’t exist at all without the efforts of Claudia Jones.

2021-02-20.title: Why Canada’s Vaccine Rollout Slowed Down
2021-02-20.abstract: The government did not promise mass inoculations during the first few months, but reductions and suspensions of shipments have created vaccine anxiety.

2021-02-20.title: Boris Johnson Calls for G7 Cooperation on Global Threats
2021-02-20.abstract: Boris Johnson, the British prime minister, hosted a virtual meeting with leaders of the Group of 7 nations on Friday and outlined the need for a multilateral approach to global vaccinations and the fight against climate change.

2021-02-20.title: Russian Court Clears Way to Send Navalny to a Penal Colony
2021-02-20.abstract: The fate of opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny remains unclear, however. He could be held in a high-security prison in Moscow for other pending legal matters.

2021-02-20.title: Finding Flowers, and Solace, in the Cracks and Grit of an Urban Jungle
2021-02-20.abstract: In densely packed Tokyo, flora is abundant in unassuming places. Hunting for it, as I learned, can lighten life’s loads.

2021-02-20.title: Tomb of Joshua, Revered Prophet, Beckons Believers in Baghdad
2021-02-20.abstract: No one knows for certain if Joshua, the Old Testament prophet, actually lived or where he might be buried. But for over 1,000 years, the sick and faithful have visited a Baghdad tomb said to be his.

2021-02-21.title: Israel Secretly Agrees to Fund Vaccines for Syria as Part of Prisoner Swap
2021-02-21.abstract: To secure the release of an Israeli civilian held in Syria, Israel agreed to finance a supply of Russian-made Covid-19 vaccines for Damascus, an official said.

2021-02-21.title: China Censors the Internet. So Why Doesn’t Russia?
2021-02-21.abstract: The Kremlin has constructed an entire infrastructure of repression but has not displaced Western apps. Instead, it is turning to outright intimidation.

2021-02-21.title: 1946: Septuplet Rumor Fools Reporters in Paris
2021-02-21.abstract: Newspaper reporters fell for a practical joke, discovering the truth only after scouring the city for a woman who had given birth to seven babies.

2021-02-21.title: Positive Coronavirus Test? Canadians Worry Their Neighbors Will Find Out
2021-02-21.abstract: The fear of public shaming is becoming so prevalent in some Canadian provinces that doctors worry it is driving virus cases underground.

2021-02-21.title: In Israeli Election, a Chance for Arabs to Gain Influence, or Lose It
2021-02-21.abstract: Jewish politicians, including Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, are courting Arab Israeli voters, and some Arab politicians are prepared to work with them.

2021-02-22.title: 10 Years After Christchurch Quake, a Hush Where 8,000 Homes Once Stood
2021-02-22.abstract: The wreckage left in New Zealand’s second-largest city was razed. Now, a swath nearly twice the size of Central Park is being reclaimed by nature.

2021-02-22.title: Lucky Luke, the Comic Book Cowboy, Discovers Race, Belatedly
2021-02-22.abstract: For the first time in the Franco-Belgian comic book classic, Black characters have full-fledged roles and are drawn without the racist depictions that marred the genre.

2021-02-22.title: The Pandemic Is Receding in the Worst Hotspots. Will It Last?
2021-02-22.abstract: Global cases are at half of their peak a month ago, offering a fragile window of opportunity as vaccinations begin to take effect.

2021-02-22.title: New York’s pop-up concerts kick off with jazz at a vaccination site.
2021-02-22.abstract: It seemed at first like a small, no-frills concert in a carefully controlled environment: the jazz musician Jon Batiste sitting at a piano in an auditorium at the Javits Convention Center on Manhattan’s West Side, performing for an audience of about 50 health care workers — some wearing scrubs, others Army fatigues.

2021-02-23.title: As Pandemic Took Hold, Suicide Rose Among Japanese Women
2021-02-23.abstract: Job losses, urban isolation, household burdens: Covid-19 has compounded the pressures on women, raising alarms in a country that has worked to reduce some of the world’s highest suicide rates.

2021-02-23.title: Afghan Civilian Casualties Soared After Peace Talks’ Start
2021-02-23.abstract: Fighting intensified in the last quarter of 2020, after talks between the Afghan government and Taliban began. Before then, it had been a less deadly year than before.

2021-02-23.title: State lawmakers defy governors in a Covid-era battle for power.
2021-02-23.abstract: State lawmakers across the country, most of them Republicans, are moving aggressively to strip the powers of governors, often Democrats, who have taken on extraordinary authority to limit the spread of the coronavirus for nearly a year.

2021-02-23.title: As the pandemic took hold, suicide rose among Japanese women.
2021-02-23.abstract: While the pandemic has been difficult for many in Japan, the pressures have been compounded for women. As in many countries, more women have lost their jobs. In Tokyo, the country’s largest metropolis, about one in five women live alone, and the exhortations to stay home and avoid visiting relatives have exacerbated feelings of isolation.

2021-02-24.title: Fading Sri Lankan Hopes for Justice Rest on U.N. Rights Council
2021-02-24.abstract: The government, strongly tied to the military, is blocking inquiries into wartime atrocities, undermining efforts to learn the fates of thousands of missing people.

2021-02-24.title: Deaths Fell in Japan Last Year. How?
2021-02-24.abstract: The decline indicated that methods for fighting the coronavirus have also been effective at curbing other illnesses.

2021-02-24.title: Disabled Apartment Owners Fear Fire Traps in Aftermath of Grenfell Disaster
2021-02-24.abstract: Years after the Grenfell Tower fire in London killed 72 people, disabled residents of high-rises worry whether they would be able to evacuate if needed. “We’ve been an afterthought,” one activist says.

2021-02-24.title: German Court Convicts Former Syrian Official of Crimes Against Humanity
2021-02-24.abstract: Eyad al-Gharib, a former secret police officer, was sentenced to four and a half years in prison in a case that rights groups have hailed as a landmark.

2021-02-25.title: California has now lost more people to Covid-19 than any other state in the U.S.
2021-02-25.abstract: A bleak reminder that the recent progress the state has made against the pandemic may be fragile.

2021-02-25.title: Ghana Receives First Vaccine Shipment Through Covax
2021-02-25.abstract: Ghana on Wednesday became the first country to receive a shipment of coronavirus vaccines through the World Health Organization’s Covax distribution program.

2021-02-25.title: Squealing Children and Noisy Neighbors? There’s a Map for That
2021-02-25.abstract: The crowdsourced guide collects anonymous gripes and pins every grievance on an interactive map, creating a record of the irritating sounds and sights of Japan.

2021-02-25.title: ‘V.I.P. Immunization’ for the Powerful and Their Cronies Rattles South America
2021-02-25.abstract: A wave of corruption scandals is exposing how the powerful and well-connected in South America jumped the line to get vaccines early. Public dismay is turning into anger.

2021-02-26.title: ‘Victim Blaming’ and Sex Education in the Boys’ Club of Australian Politics
2021-02-26.abstract: Scott Morrison’s words, critics said, revealed a disturbing sentiment.

2021-02-26.title: At Least Eight Killed in Haiti Prison Break
2021-02-26.abstract: One of Haiti’s most powerful gang leaders was among the inmates who fled the prison in Port-au-Prince.

2021-02-26.title: Governors relax restrictions as infection numbers fall.
2021-02-26.abstract: With the coronavirus slowly receding, governors around the United States are beginning to relax pandemic restrictions.

2021-02-26.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-02-26.abstract: Germany’s hoard of unused vaccine doses.

2021-02-26.title: Michael Somare, Papua New Guinea’s ‘Father of the Nation,’ Dies at 84
2021-02-26.abstract: Mr. Somare, who played a major role in leading the country to independence from Australia, was its longest-serving prime minister.

2021-02-27.title: ‘Guernica’ Tapestry Is Taken Back From U.N. by a Rockefeller
2021-02-27.abstract: The replica of Picasso’s famed painting depicting the horrors of war had hung outside the Security Council chamber since 1985, on loan from the Rockefeller family.

2021-02-27.title: Canada Approves AstraZeneca Vaccine
2021-02-27.abstract: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced on Friday that Canada’s drug regulator had authorized the AstraZeneca vaccine as well as the version produced by the Serum Institute of India.

2021-02-27.title: China Tried to Slow Divorces by Making Couples Wait. Instead, They Rushed.
2021-02-27.abstract: A new rule requiring a cooling-off period before a divorce could be granted led to an outcry, as well as a surge of applications to beat the deadline.

2021-02-27.title: Rapper’s Arrest Awakens Rage in Spanish Youth Chafing in Pandemic
2021-02-27.abstract: Nearly two weeks of sometimes violent demonstrations have turned into a collective outcry from young adults who see bleak futures and precious time lost to lockdowns.

2021-02-27.title: How to Unite a Deeply Divided Kosovo? Name a Lake After Trump
2021-02-27.abstract: A lighthearted suggestion by a U.S. envoy hoping to bridge a vast rift between Albanians and Serbs in Kosovo has taken on a life of its own — only to be ridiculed by local residents on both sides.

2021-02-28.title: Military Crackdown in Myanmar Escalates With Killing of Protesters
2021-02-28.abstract: At least 18 people were killed during protests, according to the United Nations, with security forces opening fire on crowds.

2021-02-28.title: Hong Kong Charges 47 Democracy Supporters With Violating Security Law
2021-02-28.abstract: Before Sunday, only a handful of people had been formally charged with breaking the law that China imposed last year, which carries a potential sentence of life in prison.

2021-02-28.title: The Ugly Divorce Between Britain and Brussels Is Just Getting Started
2021-02-28.abstract: As trade disputes pile up, the blame game evident in the early months of the split suits domestic political needs on both sides.

2021-02-28.title: Salvador’s Leader, Combative but Popular, May Tighten Grip in Elections
2021-02-28.abstract: Nayib Bukele, who has drawn criticism abroad for his strongman displays, is expected to expand his mandate in legislative elections that could deliver his party a decisive victory.

2021-02-28.title: Salvaged From 1941 Shipwreck, Letters Reveal Wartime Love and Sacrifice
2021-02-28.abstract: For over 70 years, the letters lay undisturbed in the wreckage of the S.S. Gairsoppa, sheltered from the ravages of ocean currents by well-positioned mail bags. Now, conservators are piecing together these undelivered messages from the past.
